annotations.label: Classification of finite simple groups (informal)

anchor.snippet: every finite simple group is either cyclic

annotations.note: Mathlib defines IsSimpleGroup and proves specific families simple (cyclic of prime order, alternating), but the full classification theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Jordan–Hölder theorem

anchor.snippet: The Jordan–Hölder theorem is a more precise way of stating this fact

annotations.note: CompositionSeries.jordan_holder proves uniqueness of composition factors for any JordanHolderLattice, which generalizes the group statement.

annotations.label: Classification theorem (formal statement)
annotations.note: Only the definition of simple groups and isolated simple families exist; the enumeration of all finite simple groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Structure of low 2-rank simple groups

anchor.snippet: The simple groups of low 2-rank are mostly groups of Lie type

annotations.note: No formalization of 2-rank classification results exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Feit–Thompson theorem (odd order solvable)

anchor.snippet: groups of odd order, which are all solvable by the Feit–Thompson theorem

annotations.note: The odd order theorem is not present in Mathlib (grep for Feit/Thompson finds only unrelated fixed-point-free automorphism results).

annotations.label: Simple groups of 2-rank 1

anchor.snippet: there are no simple groups of 2-rank 1 except for the cyclic group of order two

annotations.note: No Mathlib result classifies simple groups by 2-rank.

annotations.label: Schreier conjecture

anchor.snippet: The Schreier conjecture

annotations.note: Mathlib's Schreier.lean covers Schreier's index formula, not the conjecture that outer automorphism groups of simple groups are solvable.

annotations.label: Schur–Zassenhaus theorem for all groups

anchor.snippet: The Schur–Zassenhaus theorem for all groups

annotations.note: Subgroup.exists_right_complement'_of_coprime proves the full Schur–Zassenhaus theorem (complement exists for any normal subgroup of coprime order and index).

annotations.label: Fixed-point-free element of prime power order

anchor.snippet: A transitive permutation group on a finite set with more than 1 element has a fixed-point-free element

annotations.note: Mathlib's derangements files count derangements but do not prove existence of a fixed-point-free element of prime power order in transitive actions.

annotations.label: Classification of 2-transitive permutation groups

anchor.snippet: The classification of 2-transitive permutation groups

annotations.note: Multiple-transitivity infrastructure (IsMultiplyPretransitive, IsPreprimitive) is defined, but the CFSG-dependent classification of 2-transitive groups is not formalized.
